findings.description: Underlined section headings in PDF ('Introduction', 'Background', 'Results', 'Summary') are not underlined in HTML; they use larger bold styling instead.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Introduction', 'Background' as centered, underlined headings. HTML shows them as left-aligned bold headings without underlines. Headings remain visually distinct from body text.

findings.description: Map 1 image in PDF (page 5) shows the map and an overlaid property table as part of the image. In the HTML (page 3), the same image and table appear to be rendered correctly but the presentation spans differently across pages.
findings.evidence: PDF page 5 has Map 1 with the property table as a single composite image. HTML renders this similarly with the same content visible.

findings.description: Map 2 in PDF page 7 is a single large image with full legend. HTML page 4 splits the rendering across what appears to be two partial views before showing the full map.
findings.evidence: PDF shows one complete Map 2 image. HTML shows a partial crop at the bottom of one section and then the full map image below.

findings.description: Diagram 1 in PDF (page 13) shows three Biobase screenshots arranged side by side and below. HTML (page 9) shows them stacked vertically in sequence.
findings.evidence: PDF has Zone 1&2 combined on left, WRC Boat Ramp on right, North shoreline below. HTML shows them in a single-column vertical stack.

findings.description: The HTML has a nested <main> element, resulting in duplicate structural wrapping. While not content duplication per se, it creates two <main> tags which is semantically incorrect.
